weather_report:
Subiaco wakes beneath a polished blue, the late-morning sun bright but gentler than summer, casting clean shadows and sharpening every edge. The air feels mild and dry, a day made for simple errands and small pleasures, with clear skies holding steady into the afternoon. UV will swell toward midday despite the softer season, so a brimmed hat, sunglasses, and a smear of sunscreen will serve you well; light layers will carry you from a coolish shade to a warm patch of pavement without fuss. Expect a lucid, untroubled sky and good visibility, the kind of clarity that invites unhurried looks at window displays and jacaranda-less avenues dreaming of spring.

Take the long way to lunch, sit in the gentle light of a courtyard, or walk a quiet loop past the shops and verandas, letting the day do its easy work. Air out the house, hang a wash, water the pots, and if you must be indoors, park yourself by a window and let the blue keep you company.
